methodsAttempted.query: site:uidaho.edu generative AI policy OR guidance students faculty University of Idaho
methodsAttempted.notes: Found the official AI hub, an Idaho INBRE generative AI resource page, related AI news/event pages, and no direct public graduate school policy text.
Found stronger official support.uidaho.edu knowledge base sources for AI guidance, syllabus language, and an OIT/AI Execution Group memo.
Verified three public University of Idaho knowledge base articles with substantive AI guidance.
AI hub and AI in Teaching page were useful for discovery but either generic or linked to intranet/SharePoint content for substantive teaching guidance.

artifacts.summary: Official University of Idaho public knowledge base pages provided substantive AI data-handling, approved-service, agentic-AI, syllabus-language, and employee memo evidence. The AI hub, AI in Teaching page, SharePoint/CETL intranet link, and Idaho INBRE page were recorded as discovery context or rejected/limited sources because they were generic, login-gated, or not specific enough to support University of Idaho policy claims.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Public University of Idaho knowledge base article with AI service, data classification, output handling, IP, and agentic AI guidance.
Public University of Idaho knowledge base article reproducing Center for Teaching and Learning AI syllabus language options.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Memo from OIT and AI Execution Group
artifacts.verificationNotes: Public University of Idaho knowledge base memo from OIT and the AI Execution Group, effective Fall 2025.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:uidaho.edu generative AI policy OR guidance students faculty University of Idaho
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official AI hub is useful for discovery but mostly links to events/news and contains insufficient direct policy text.
Official page contained only links to a CETL SharePoint page and sample syllabus wording; substantive linked content required login or was found separately in the public support KB.
Fetch redirected to Microsoft sign-in; no login wall was bypassed.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:uidaho.edu generative AI policy OR guidance students faculty University of Idaho
artifacts.verificationNotes: Idaho INBRE page links to University of Idaho AI teaching resources and gives examples from other Idaho institutions, but the substantive syllabus examples are not University of Idaho policy claims.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: AI in Research Coming Soon; AI Literacy & Ethics Coming Soon; AI Showcase Coming Soon.
artifacts.notes: Official hub retained for discovery only; direct claim evidence came from support.uidaho.edu knowledge base pages.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: AI in Teaching page links to Center for Excellence in Teaching and Learning and Sample Syllabi Wording on AI Use.
artifacts.notes: Page did not itself contain enough substantive guidance to support a claim.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Fetch result showed a Microsoft sign-in page with title 'Sign in to your account'.
The page lists links to Idaho institution policies/guidance and includes syllabus examples from Boise State, College of Southern Idaho, and Lewis-Clark State College.
artifacts.notes: Official University of Idaho-hosted resource, but the substantive examples were from other institutions and not used as University of Idaho policy evidence.

targets.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Memo from OIT and AI Execution Group

stopConditions: Stop after verified public official University of Idaho sources support narrow claim/evidence candidates.
Do not bypass SharePoint or Microsoft sign-in for intranet/CETL material.
Do not infer university-wide student conduct policy from examples or event pages.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Memo from OIT and AI Execution Group

artifacts.claimText: University of Idaho OIT guidance says data uploaded to or downloaded from AI, including prompts, is subject to APM 30.11 data classification, and that listed AI services are approved only for the indicated data classifications.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Idaho OIT guidance allows unapproved, non-banned AI providers only when usage does not violate terms, copyright, or license requirements, uses low-risk data not specific to the university, and includes adequate review of terms of service and AI output.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Idaho OIT guidance says agentic AI tools should not receive credentials to university systems, should not be installed on systems with moderate- or high-risk data access, and should be tested in a sandboxed workflow.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Idaho Center for Teaching and Learning syllabus language presents three course-level AI options: prohibiting AI use, allowing AI only with prior instructor permission, or allowing AI with documentation and citation.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: University of Idaho's OIT and AI Execution Group memo tells university employees not to put identifiable student, employee, or regulated data into public AI models like ChatGPT and to remain vigilant about FERPA, HIPAA, GLBA, and other compliance requirements when using AI.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim about OIT AI data-classification guidance, not as a full university-wide AI policy.
Approved because the claim follows the conditional wording in the source and does not overstate unapproved AI use.
Approved as security guidance using the source's should-not phrasing.
Approved as teaching guidance/sample syllabus language only; it is not presented as a binding university-wide student policy.
Approved as employee-facing memo guidance about public AI models and regulated data.

artifacts.title: University of Idaho AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Draft evidence package for University of Idaho based on official public knowledge base sources covering AI data classification, approved and unapproved services, agentic AI safeguards, sample syllabus language, and employee/faculty AI memo guidance.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
CETL/SharePoint material encountered during discovery redirected to Microsoft sign-in and was not accessed.
The AI hub and event pages mention additional activity and a graduate school AI policy, but this run did not locate a direct public policy page for that policy.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
